skill.name: creating-skills
skill.description: Guides users through creating Claude Code skills with clear frontmatter, focused instructions, naming rules, helper script guidance, and quality checks.
skill.summary: Create well-structured Claude Code skills with focused instructions and quality checks.
skill.author: Bae-ChangHyun

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, filesystem, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found the command and cryptography alerts are markdown examples or text matches. The only real behavior described is user-directed creation of skill files under ~/.claude/skills, which is expected for this skill but should remain explicit and user approved.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: The 47 external command alerts occur in fenced markdown examples, inline markdown, and permission pattern examples. No runnable script file or automatic command execution is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside markdown code fences, tables, and inline examples. There is no separate executable script or instruction to run commands without user intent.

low_findings.title: User-Directed Hidden Directory File Creation
low_findings.description: The skill shows mkdir and cat examples for ~/.claude/skills and displays that directory in a structure diagram. This is expected for skill authoring, but it writes under a hidden home directory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem target is explicit and relevant to Claude skill installation. Risk remains low because the examples are user-visible and do not hide persistence or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The high-severity cryptography alerts match documentation words and placeholders, including description text. No evidence found of DES, MD5, SHA-1, encryption, hashing, or credential handling code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain prose, checklist text, or placeholders, not cryptographic APIs. The scanner appears to have matched substrings such as desc or description.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alert Is Section Text
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert points to an anti-patterns section heading and table. No evidence found of host inspection, environment probing, or data collection behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The location is a markdown table about skill design anti-patterns. It contains no commands that inspect the system or gather local information.

content.value_statement: Building a useful AI skill requires clear triggers, focused instructions, and safe structure. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through skill naming, SKILL.md content, helper scripts, and quality checks.
actual_capabilities: Explains required SKILL.md frontmatter fields for skill discovery.
Shows a quick start for creating a skill directory and main skill file.
Defines naming rules for lowercase, hyphenated skill names.
Gives guidance on when helper scripts add value.
Describes progressive disclosure using references and focused SKILL.md content.
Provides a quality checklist for reviewing a skill before use.
limitations: Does not install or publish skills automatically.
Does not validate generated skills with a test runner.
Does not include runnable helper scripts beyond examples.
Focuses on Claude Code conventions, not every AI agent platform.

use_cases.title: Draft a New Internal Skill
use_cases.description: Create a focused SKILL.md outline with triggers, rules, and a compact workflow for a team process.

use_cases.title: Review Skill Quality
use_cases.description: Check an existing skill for naming, frontmatter, token budget, helper script value, and clear instructions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Skill Authoring
use_cases.description: Give contributors a consistent structure for writing marketplace-ready Claude Code skills.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a Claude Code skill for [task]. Include the name, description, core workflow, and important rules.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Skill Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill description and improve its trigger phrases, scope, and discoverability while keeping it concise.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Skill Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this SKILL.md draft against the quality checklist. Identify missing fields, unclear rules, and unnecessary detail.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Skill Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a complete skill package for [workflow]. Decide what belongs in SKILL.md, references, and optional helper scripts.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for handling release notes.
output_examples.output: A concise skill outline with a lowercase hyphenated name, discovery description, workflow sections, and release-note quality rules.

output_examples.input: Review this existing SKILL.md for marketplace readiness.
output: Summary of missing or weak frontmatter.
Notes on unclear trigger phrases and overlong sections.
A short checklist of changes needed before publishing.

output_examples.input: Decide whether my skill needs helper scripts.
output_examples.output: A recommendation that separates reusable complex logic from simple commands that should stay inline.

best_practices: Keep SKILL.md focused on the workflow and move long examples into references.
Use clear trigger phrases that match how users ask for help.
Add helper scripts only when they provide reusable logic or structured output.
anti_patterns: Do not create wrapper scripts for simple one-line commands.
Do not include multiple competing workflows in one skill.
Do not over-explain basic concepts that the AI already understands.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create Claude Code skills with clear frontmatter, instructions, naming, and review checks.

faq.question: Does it write the skill files for me?
faq.answer: It provides structure and examples. File creation should happen only when the user asks for it.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is useful for Codex-assisted skill drafting and review.

faq.question: Does it include marketplace security review?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on authoring quality, not full marketplace security analysis.

faq.question: When should I add reference files?
faq.answer: Add reference files when examples or details would make SKILL.md too long.

faq.question: When should I add helper scripts?
faq.answer: Add helper scripts for reusable complex logic, structured processing, or repeated calculations.
